mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations despite rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a t-test against baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad demand contraction that suppresses corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a classification accuracy above 55%, or the interaction term loses significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply chain bottlenecks that trigger short-term market repricing, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered observations, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a positive 20-day return in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >65% of observations despite payroll growth, or the Rank IC falls below -0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden surge in real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% annualized signals an overheating economy that prompts anticipatory risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for market volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a robust supply-demand balance that boosts corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the predictive accuracy drops below chance levels during mid-cycle expansion periods.
